content:
1	---
2	name: blueprint-writer
3	description: Update one blueprint chapter to reflect strategy changes, fill missing definitions or theorems, or align prose with the current Lean structure. Plan-agent-dispatched; one writer per chapter. May spawn reference-retriever mid-session when drafting reveals a missing source.
4	write_domain: "blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex"
5	read_only: false
6	can_spawn: true
7	default_enabled: true
8	dispatcher_notes: |
9	  - Dispatch one writer per chapter that the blueprint-reviewer's
10	    checklist flags as incomplete, lacking proof detail, or missing
11	    multi-route coverage.
12	  - Each directive must be precise: strategy context (the slice that
13	    matters for this chapter), required definitions/theorems (with
14	    enough mathematical detail to formalize), references, and
15	    explicit out-of-scope items. Writers do NOT speculate beyond
16	    what the directive lists.
17	  - **Authorize the retriever in the writer's --write-domain.** If
18	    the chapter might need fresh source material, dispatch the
19	    writer with TWO globs:
20	      --write-domain 'blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter>.tex'
21	      --write-domain 'references/**'
22	    The writer itself only edits its assigned chapter (its prompt
23	    body enforces that), but the second glob authorizes a child
24	    reference-retriever if the writer discovers it needs one. Omit
25	    `references/**` only when you are confident the writer will not
26	    need new sources (e.g. purely cleanup edits).
27	  - If the strategy has multiple viable routes and one route's chapters
28	    are missing, dispatch a writer per route to bring all routes to
29	    parity before any prover work begins. Do not push provers onto a
30	    route whose blueprint coverage is incomplete.
31	  - After a significant writer round, re-dispatch blueprint-reviewer
32	    in the same iteration to confirm the updated blueprint is now
33	    sufficient — do not assume the writer fixed everything.
34	  - If a writer's report contains entries under "Strategy-modifying
35	    findings", STOP and update STRATEGY.md before any further Lean
36	    work this iter. The writer is telling you the prose surfaced a
37	    strategy-level issue.
38	  - If a writer's report includes child reference-retriever dispatches,
39	    skim the new `references/<slug>.md` files before using the
40	    writer's output — the writer relied on them, so should you.
41	---
42	
43	# Blueprint Writer
44	
45	You write or revise **one blueprint chapter** under plan-agent direction. You receive a precise directive naming the target chapter, the strategy context the chapter must reflect, the definitions/theorems that must be present, and the scope of changes you may make.
46	
47	## Your Job
48	
49	The plan agent has decided that a specific blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex`) needs to change — to reflect a strategic decision, fill a missing definition or theorem the project needs, or align the informal prose with the current Lean structure. Your directive tells you which chapter, what must be there, and what counts as out-of-scope.
50	
51	You only edit the **one chapter named in your directive**. Your declared `--write-domain` should reflect that (`blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex`). You do NOT edit other chapters, `.lean` files, or `content.tex`. Cross-chapter inconsistencies you spot go in the report's "Notes for Plan Agent" section — you flag them, you don't fix them.
52	
53	## Directive Format
54	
55	```markdown
56	# Blueprint Writer Directive
57	
58	## Slug
59	<slug>
60	
61	## Target chapter
62	blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter-slug>.tex
63	
64	## Strategy context
65	<the relevant slice of STRATEGY.md the plan agent extracted for you — typically a paragraph or two describing what this chapter must support in the overall arc. You do NOT read STRATEGY.md yourself; the plan agent gives you only what is relevant.>
66	
67	## Required content
68	- Definition <name>: <informal description of what it must define, with the mathematical content the prover needs in order to formalize>
69	- Theorem <name>: <statement + intent of the proof sketch the prover should formalize>
70	- Proof sketch for <theorem>: <how detailed; what cross-references; whether to expand a step into sub-lemmas>
71	- ...
72	
73	## Out of scope
74	<things that look related but the plan agent does NOT want you to touch this round>
75	
76	## References
77	- <path/to/reference.md or arxiv ID>: <which sections are relevant>
78	- ...
79	
80	## Expected outcome
81	<what the chapter should look like after, in one paragraph>
82	```
83	
84	If a section is omitted from your directive, the plan agent decided you don't need it. Don't speculate beyond what was given.
85	
86	## Chapter format
87	
88	Each declaration block in a chapter looks like:
89	
90	```latex
91	\begin{theorem}[name_for_humans]
92	  \label{thm:some_label}
93	  \lean{namespace.theorem_name}
94	  \uses{def:related_definition, lem:supporting_lemma}
95	  Informal statement of the theorem, using standard mathematical notation.
96	\end{theorem}
97	
98	\begin{proof}
99	  \uses{thm:another_result}
100	  Step-by-step informal proof. Reference blueprint labels with \uses{...}
101	  so the dependency graph stays accurate.
102	\end{proof}
103	```
104	
105	Use `\definition`, `\lemma`, `\theorem`, `\proposition`, `\corollary` as appropriate. `\lean{...}` names the Lean declaration this block corresponds to. `\uses{...}` records cross-references; keep it accurate so `leanblueprint`'s dependency graph remains usable.
106	
107	## Rules
108	
109	### What you CAN do
110	- Add new declaration blocks (definitions, lemmas, theorems, propositions, corollaries) under direction.
111	- Expand or revise existing prose / proof sketches in your assigned chapter.
112	- Add `\uses{...}` cross-references.
113	- Adjust `\lean{...}` hints when the directive names a new Lean target.
114	
115	### What you MUST do
116	- **Keep the chapter valid LaTeX.** Don't leave dangling `\begin{...}` without matching `\end{...}`. Compile-checking is the plan agent's responsibility but you must not introduce syntax errors.
117	- **Stay within your chapter.** Your declared write-domain is one `*.tex` file. The Archon CLI rejects writes outside it.
118	- **Define non-standard macros in `blueprint/src/macros/common.tex`** before using them — but: that file is outside your write-domain, so you DON'T touch it. If the directive requires a new macro, you note in your report "needs macro `\foo`" and leave the LaTeX using the new command name; the plan agent adds the macro before next iter's typeset.
119	- **Use mathematical, not Lean-syntactic prose.** Describe the proof in the language of mathematics — definitions, set inclusions, ring maps, universal properties — not in Lean tactic syntax. The prover formalizes your math.
120	- **Document every change** in your report.
121	
122	### What you MUST NOT do
123	- **Do NOT add `\leanok` or `\mathlibok` markers.** Those are managed by the `sync_leanok` phase + the review agent — never by you.
124	- **Do NOT edit other chapters.** Even when you spot a related issue, flag it in "Notes for Plan Agent" instead of fixing it.
125	- **Do NOT edit `content.tex`** (the top-level blueprint file that `\input`s the chapters).
126	- **Do NOT edit `.lean` files** or any other state file.
127	- **Do NOT write Lean syntax** — keep the chapter mathematical, not syntactic.
128	- **Do NOT expand scope.** Stick to what the directive listed under "Required content".
129	
130	## Reading references
131	
132	You write mathematics, not literature criticism — your prose must be grounded in authoritative sources, not your training memory. Before composing any new declaration block:
133	
134	1. **Read `references/summary.md`** to see the index of sources the project already has. Sources directly relevant to your chapter are your first stop.
135	2. **Read every reference file your directive names** under its `## References` section. The plan agent named those because the chapter needs them.
136	3. **Read anything in `references/summary.md` your directive didn't name but that is clearly relevant** to your chapter (same area, same theorem, same construction).
137	
138	Do NOT write content from your training memory when you could ground it in a reference instead. When the references say one thing and your memory says another, trust the references.
139	
140	## Dispatching a reference-retriever (when the project's sources don't have what you need)
141	
142	If, while drafting, you discover that the chapter needs material the project's `references/` doesn't contain, **dispatch a `reference-retriever` mid-session** rather than guessing or papering over the gap.
143	
144	Conditions for dispatch:
145	
146	- The directive named a source (`Smith 2018`, an arXiv ID) that isn't in `references/` yet.
147	- The required content is in a known textbook (Hartshorne, Vakil, Stacks Project, etc.) but the project hasn't summarized the relevant chapter.
148	- You need a Mathlib-adjacent source (nLab, math overflow, Stacks Project) the directive didn't anticipate.
149	
150	Dispatch (Bash, foreground, in your write-domain only if it includes `references/**`):
151	
152	```
153	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
154	  --name reference-retriever \
155	  --slug <kebab-slug-for-the-source> \
156	  --directive-file .archon/logs/iter-NNN/<your-slug>/reference-retriever-<child-slug>-directive.md \
157	  --write-domain 'references/**'
158	```
159	
160	Write the directive file first; the directive format is documented in `.archon/subagents/reference-retriever.md`. The retriever returns when the new summary is on disk; **then resume writing**, citing the new reference.
161	
162	If your invocation's recorded write-domain does NOT include `references/**` (your `parent.write_domain` in `dispatch.jsonl`), the dispatch will be rejected. In that case, STOP writing the affected section, report the missing source in "Notes for Plan Agent", and finish the parts of the chapter you can still write. The plan agent will re-dispatch you next iter with the broader write-domain.
163	
164	## Workflow
165	
166	1. Read your directive completely.
167	2. **Read `references/summary.md`** and every reference your directive points at; also read sibling-chapter material that informs cross-references.
168	3. Read the target chapter currently on disk to see what's already there.
169	4. Plan the edits: which blocks to add, which to revise. Decide where each new block goes in the chapter's existing flow.
170	5. **If a needed source is missing from `references/`, dispatch a `reference-retriever`** (see above) and wait for it to return before drafting the affected sections.
171	6. Make the edits.
172	7. Verify the file is still valid LaTeX at a glance (no unmatched begin/end, balanced braces in `\label`/`\uses`/`\lean`).
173	8. Write your report.
174	
175	## Logging
176	
177	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/blueprint-writer-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path under `task_results/<parent-slug>/` when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
178	
179	```markdown
180	# Blueprint Writer Report
181	
182	## Slug
183	<slug>
184	
185	## Status
186	<COMPLETE | INCOMPLETE>
187	<If INCOMPLETE: which required items could not be written and why.>
188	
189	## Target chapter
190	blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter-slug>.tex
191	
192	## Changes Made
193	- **Added definition** `\definition`/`\label{def:foo}`/`\lean{Foo.bar}` — <one line on what it captures>
194	- **Added theorem** `\theorem`/`\label{thm:foo}` — <one line on statement>
195	  - Proof sketch added: <Y/N + brief shape>
196	- **Revised** `<existing label>` — <one line on what changed>
197	- ...
198	
199	## Cross-references introduced
200	- `\uses{thm:bar}` added in proof of `\thm:foo` — verify `thm:bar` exists in <chapter or this same one>
201	- ...
202	
203	## Macros needed (if any)
204	- `\foo{...}` — used in <where>; needs definition in `macros/common.tex`. NOT added by me (out of write-domain).
205	
206	## Reference-retriever dispatches (if any)
207	- slug `<child-slug>`: requested `<source>`. Status: COMPLETE / NOT_FOUND / PARTIAL. Summary at `references/<slug>.md`.
208	- ...
209	
210	## Notes for Plan Agent
211	- <any inconsistency I noticed in sibling chapters that the directive didn't cover>
212	- <any structural concern: e.g. "the chapter is now 800 lines, consider splitting">
213	- <any reference I had to guess at because the directive's reference section was incomplete>
214	
215	## Strategy-modifying findings
216	<populate this section ONLY if writing the chapter surfaced a need to
217	change the project's strategy itself — not just the chapter content.
218	Examples:
219	- A definition you were asked to write turns out to require a property
220	  that the strategy assumes is automatic but is not.
221	- A theorem you sketched is provable as stated, but its consequence
222	  used elsewhere in STRATEGY.md does not follow from it.
223	- A reference cited as authoritative actually requires a different
224	  setup than the strategy assumes.
225	
226	When this section is non-empty, the plan agent must update STRATEGY.md
227	before any further Lean work this iteration. Do NOT silently adapt the
228	chapter to paper over a strategy-level issue — surface it here.>
229	```
230	
231	## Return Value
232	
233	Your final assistant message:
234	
235	- One line: `<slug>: COMPLETE | INCOMPLETE — <one-sentence outcome>`
236	- The path to your full report.
237	
238	Keep the inline return short. The plan agent reads the full report.
239
Now I have what I need. Let me check the analogies file briefly and verify my understanding before editing.

c1-route.md
cech-koszul-precedent.md
finite-product-localisation-and-cech-r-linearity.md

1	# Analogy: Mathlib idiom for the Picard group of a scheme as units of a monoidal-category skeleton
2	
3	## Slug
4	c1-route
5	
6	## Iteration
7	108
8	
9	## Question
10	
11	What is the canonical Mathlib idiom for defining the **Picard group of a
12	scheme as the units of a monoidal category of `O_X`-modules**, and is that
13	idiom executable today against the project's already-shipped
14	`MonoidalCategory (X.Modules)` / `BraidedCategory (X.Modules)` chain
15	(which transitively depends on the deferred `instIsMonoidal_W` sorry for
16	`(W X).IsMonoidal`)?
17	
18	## Project artifact(s)
19	
20	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/LineBundle.lean:85-122` — current
21	  `LineBundle X := CommRing.Pic Γ(X, ⊤)` and `Pic.pullback` via
22	  `CommRing.Pic.mapRingHom`.
23	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Modules/Monoidal.lean:166-194` — the deferred
24	  `instIsMonoidal_W` sorry plus the `instMonoidalCategoryStruct` and
25	  `instMonoidalCategory` instances that transitively consume it.
26	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/Functor.lean:108-174` — `quotMap` and
27	  `PicardFunctor`, parametric in `Pic.pullback`.
28	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FunctorAb.lean` — additive-group flavor
29	  of the same construction.
30	
31	## Decisions identified
32	
33	The directive's single design question compresses four orthogonal decisions.
34	
35	### Decision A: name of the "invertible-objects" type
36	
37	- **Mathlib idiom**: there is **no type literally named
38	  `MonoidalCategory.Invertible`** in Mathlib b80f227. The canonical idiom is
39	  `(Skeleton C)ˣ` — the **units of the (skeleton's) monoid structure on the
40	  objects of a (braided) monoidal category**. Specifically:
41	  - `CategoryTheory.Skeleton.instMonoid` (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Skeleton:60`)
42	    promotes `Skeleton C` to `Monoid` from `[MonoidalCategory C]`.
43	  - `CategoryTheory.Skeleton.instCommMonoid` (`...:80`) promotes it to
44	    `CommMonoid` from `[BraidedCategory C]`.
45	  - `(Skeleton C)ˣ` is then automatically `CommGroup` from `instCommGroupUnits`.
46	  - The ring precedent (`CommRing.Pic`,
47	    `Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup:407-408`) defines
48	    `def CommRing.Pic (R) := Shrink (Skeleton (SemimoduleCat R))ˣ`.
49	
50	  Why Mathlib chose this: skeleton-level units neatly factor the

Now I have full context. Let me make the three edits.

**Edit 1**: Update L38 `\uses{...}` in the `\thm:Pic_representable` proof block to include both oracle theorems.

The file /home/archon/FormalizationProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_Functor.tex has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)
**Edit 2**: Update L74 "Inherited (post-C1) from Chapter Picard_LineBundle" bullet to record both gaps as a pair.

input.old_string: \item \emph{Inherited (post-C1) from Chapter~\ref{chap:Picard_LineBundle}.} Monoidality of the categorical pull-back functor on sheaves of $\mathcal O_X$-modules. Mathlib b80f227 does not register a $\mathrm{Functor.Monoidal}\,(\mathrm{Scheme.Modules.pullback}\,f)$ instance; the project surfaces the missing iso $f^*(M \otimes_{\mathcal O_Y} N) \cong f^* M \otimes_{\mathcal O_X} f^* N$ as the named-deferred Theorem~\ref{thm:SheafOfModules_pullback_tensorObj} in \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/LineBundle.lean}. This sorry is inherited by $\mathrm{Pic.pullback}$ (= $f^*$ on the Picard group, Theorem~\ref{thm:Scheme_Pic_pullback}), which Definition~\ref{def:Pic_functor} consumes through the projection-pull-back subgroup $p_S^*\Pic(S)$.
input.new_string: \item \emph{Inherited (post-C1) from Chapter~\ref{chap:Picard_LineBundle}.} Monoidality of the categorical pull-back functor on sheaves of $\mathcal O_X$-modules. Mathlib b80f227 does not register a $\mathrm{Functor.Monoidal}\,(\mathrm{Scheme.Modules.pullback}\,f)$ instance; the project records this absence as a \emph{pair} of named-deferred Theorems in \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/LineBundle.lean}, namely the tensor-preservation iso $f^*(M \otimes_{\mathcal O_Y} N) \cong f^* M \otimes_{\mathcal O_X} f^* N$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:SheafOfModules_pullback_tensorObj}, the $\mu$-iso) and the unit-preservation iso $f^*(\mathcal O_Y) \cong \mathcal O_X$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:SheafOfModules_pullback_oneIso}, the $\varepsilon$-iso). These two siblings collapse simultaneously when a future Mathlib refresh lands $(\mathrm{SheafOfModules.pullback}\,f).\mathrm{Monoidal}$, at which point the pair is replaced by the canonical $\mathrm{MonoidalCategoryStruct.tensorObj}$-comparison and $\mathrm{Monoidal.}\varepsilon\mathrm{Iso}$ structure fields. The pair is inherited by $\mathrm{Pic.pullback}$ (= $f^*$ on the Picard group, Theorem~\ref{thm:Scheme_Pic_pullback}), which Definition~\ref{def:Pic_functor} consumes through the projection-pull-back subgroup $p_S^*\Pic(S)$.

content: The file /home/archon/FormalizationProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_Functor.tex has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

This is a focused 3-edit job; task tracking isn't worth the overhead. Continuing.

**Edit 3**: Update L84 "Pull-back gap" bullet — replace the stale sorry-bodied prose with the iter-109 closure narrative.

input.old_string: \item \emph{Pull-back gap.} The Lean body of $\mathrm{Pic.pullback}$ (\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/LineBundle.lean:93}) is currently \texttt{sorry}, pending closure of (or a hand-construction equivalent to) Theorem~\ref{thm:SheafOfModules_pullback_tensorObj} (\texttt{Picard/LineBundle.lean:82}): the monoidality iso $f^*(M \otimes_{\mathcal O_Y} N) \cong f^* M \otimes_{\mathcal O_X} f^* N$ of the categorical pull-back functor $\mathrm{Scheme.Modules.pullback}\,f$ on quasi-coherent $\mathcal O$-modules. The functoriality lemmas $\mathrm{Pic.pullback\_id}$ and $\mathrm{Pic.pullback\_comp}$ (\texttt{Picard/LineBundle.lean:99,\,105}) are likewise sorry-bodied for the same reason. Definition~\ref{def:Pic_functor} consumes the pull-back via the projection subgroup $p_S^*\Pic(S)$, and Theorem~\ref{thm:Scheme_Pic_pullback} packages the dependency. The well-definedness of $\mathrm{PicardFunctor.quotMap}$ (descent of $\mathrm{Pic.pullback}\,(\mathrm{fiberMap}\,C\,f)$ through $\mathrm{QuotientGroup.lift}$) compiles because it reasons against the \emph{statements} of $\mathrm{Pic.pullback\_id}$ and $\mathrm{Pic.pullback\_comp}$ rather than against their computational unfolding.
input.new_string: \item \emph{Pull-back gap.} Post-iter-109, the Lean body of $\mathrm{Pic.pullback}$ in \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/LineBundle.lean} is \emph{closed} by a hand-construction: the underlying function is $\mathrm{Functor.mapSkeleton}\,(\mathrm{Scheme.Modules.pullback}\,f) \colon \mathrm{Skeleton}\,Y.\mathrm{Modules} \to \mathrm{Skeleton}\,X.\mathrm{Modules}$ (Mathlib's lift of an arbitrary functor to skeleta), bundled into a $\mathrm{MonoidHom}$ whose $\mathrm{map\_mul'}$ obligation is discharged by Theorem~\ref{thm:SheafOfModules_pullback_tensorObj} (the $\mu$-iso $f^*(M \otimes_{\mathcal O_Y} N) \cong f^* M \otimes_{\mathcal O_X} f^* N$) and whose $\mathrm{map\_one'}$ obligation is discharged by Theorem~\ref{thm:SheafOfModules_pullback_oneIso} (the $\varepsilon$-iso $f^*(\mathcal O_Y) \cong \mathcal O_X$); the final $f^* \colon \Pic(Y) \to \Pic(X)$ is then $\mathrm{Units.map}$ of that $\mathrm{MonoidHom}$. The closure transitively consumes the pair $\{\text{Theorem~\ref{thm:SheafOfModules_pullback_tensorObj}},\;\text{Theorem~\ref{thm:SheafOfModules_pullback_oneIso}}\}$ and no other Mathlib gap on the pull-back side. The functoriality lemmas $\mathrm{Pic.pullback\_id}$ and $\mathrm{Pic.pullback\_comp}$ are themselves closed in iter-109 using Mathlib's existing $\mathrm{Scheme.Modules.pullbackId}$ and $\mathrm{Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp}$ together with the same skeleton-of-iso projection used in $\mathrm{Pic.pullback}$; they add no further oracle dependency beyond what $\mathrm{Pic.pullback}$ already pulls in. Definition~\ref{def:Pic_functor} consumes the pull-back via the projection subgroup $p_S^*\Pic(S)$, and Theorem~\ref{thm:Scheme_Pic_pullback} packages the dependency. The well-definedness of $\mathrm{PicardFunctor.quotMap}$ (descent of $\mathrm{Pic.pullback}\,(\mathrm{fiberMap}\,C\,f)$ through $\mathrm{QuotientGroup.lift}$) compiles cleanly against the now-closed equalities $\mathrm{Pic.pullback\_id}$ and $\mathrm{Pic.pullback\_comp}$, rather than against opaque sorries.

content: The file /home/archon/FormalizationProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_Functor.tex has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)
Now let me verify the file is still valid LaTeX and the edits read cleanly.

70	
71	What is \emph{missing} and supplied by this chapter (as a deferred sorry):
72	\begin{itemize}
73	  \item The actual representability theorem (Theorem~\ref{thm:Pic_representable}). FGA-level, kept as a sorry; all four Jacobian.lean sorries reduce to it once it is closed.
74	  \item \emph{Inherited (post-C1) from Chapter~\ref{chap:Picard_LineBundle}.} Monoidality of the categorical pull-back functor on sheaves of $\mathcal O_X$-modules. Mathlib b80f227 does not register a $\mathrm{Functor.Monoidal}\,(\mathrm{Scheme.Modules.pullback}\,f)$ instance; the project records this absence as a \emph{pair} of named-deferred Theorems in \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/LineBundle.lean}, namely the tensor-preservation iso $f^*(M \otimes_{\mathcal O_Y} N) \cong f^* M \otimes_{\mathcal O_X} f^* N$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:SheafOfModules_pullback_tensorObj}, the $\mu$-iso) and the unit-preservation iso $f^*(\mathcal O_Y) \cong \mathcal O_X$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:SheafOfModules_pullback_oneIso}, the $\varepsilon$-iso). These two siblings collapse simultaneously when a future Mathlib refresh lands $(\mathrm{SheafOfModules.pullback}\,f).\mathrm{Monoidal}$, at which point the pair is replaced by the canonical $\mathrm{MonoidalCategoryStruct.tensorObj}$-comparison and $\mathrm{Monoidal.}\varepsilon\mathrm{Iso}$ structure fields. The pair is inherited by $\mathrm{Pic.pullback}$ (= $f^*$ on the Picard group, Theorem~\ref{thm:Scheme_Pic_pullback}), which Definition~\ref{def:Pic_functor} consumes through the projection-pull-back subgroup $p_S^*\Pic(S)$.
75	\end{itemize}
76	
77	\subsection{Post-C1 dependency note}
78	
79	After the Phase~C1 refactor (iter-109), $\mathrm{LineBundle}\,X$ is the units of the skeleton of the symmetric monoidal category $X.\mathrm{Modules}$ (Chapter~\ref{chap:Picard_LineBundle}), matching the geometric definition of "invertible quasi-coherent $\mathcal O_X$-module modulo isomorphism" on schemes of every shape. The global-sections-approximation caveat that previously qualified $\PicardFunctor$ on non-affine $S$ is therefore no longer in force; the $\PicardFunctor(S)$ values are now isomorphism classes of invertible $\mathcal O_{C \times_k S}$-modules in the correct sense.
80	
81	The $\PicardFunctor$ construction nonetheless transitively consumes two named-deferred sorries, both surfaced in Chapter~\ref{chap:Picard_LineBundle}.
82	
83	\begin{itemize}
84	  \item \emph{Pull-back gap.} Post-iter-109, the Lean body of $\mathrm{Pic.pullback}$ in \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/LineBundle.lean} is \emph{closed} by a hand-construction: the underlying function is $\mathrm{Functor.mapSkeleton}\,(\mathrm{Scheme.Modules.pullback}\,f) \colon \mathrm{Skeleton}\,Y.\mathrm{Modules} \to \mathrm{Skeleton}\,X.\mathrm{Modules}$ (Mathlib's lift of an arbitrary functor to skeleta), bundled into a $\mathrm{MonoidHom}$ whose $\mathrm{map\_mul'}$ obligation is discharged by Theorem~\ref{thm:SheafOfModules_pullback_tensorObj} (the $\mu$-iso $f^*(M \otimes_{\mathcal O_Y} N) \cong f^* M \otimes_{\mathcal O_X} f^* N$) and whose $\mathrm{map\_one'}$ obligation is discharged by Theorem~\ref{thm:SheafOfModules_pullback_oneIso} (the $\varepsilon$-iso $f^*(\mathcal O_Y) \cong \mathcal O_X$); the final $f^* \colon \Pic(Y) \to \Pic(X)$ is then $\mathrm{Units.map}$ of that $\mathrm{MonoidHom}$. The closure transitively consumes the pair $\{\text{Theorem~\ref{thm:SheafOfModules_pullback_tensorObj}},\;\text{Theorem~\ref{thm:SheafOfModules_pullback_oneIso}}\}$ and no other Mathlib gap on the pull-back side. The functoriality lemmas $\mathrm{Pic.pullback\_id}$ and $\mathrm{Pic.pullback\_comp}$ are themselves closed in iter-109 using Mathlib's existing $\mathrm{Scheme.Modules.pullbackId}$ and $\mathrm{Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp}$ together with the same skeleton-of-iso projection used in $\mathrm{Pic.pullback}$; they add no further oracle dependency beyond what $\mathrm{Pic.pullback}$ already pulls in. Definition~\ref{def:Pic_functor} consumes the pull-back via the projection subgroup $p_S^*\Pic(S)$, and Theorem~\ref{thm:Scheme_Pic_pullback} packages the dependency. The well-definedness of $\mathrm{PicardFunctor.quotMap}$ (descent of $\mathrm{Pic.pullback}\,(\mathrm{fiberMap}\,C\,f)$ through $\mathrm{QuotientGroup.lift}$) compiles cleanly against the now-closed equalities $\mathrm{Pic.pullback\_id}$ and $\mathrm{Pic.pullback\_comp}$, rather than against opaque sorries.
85	  \item \emph{Monoidality-of-sheafification gap.} The $\mathrm{CommGroup}\,(\Pic(X))$ structure derives from $\mathrm{BraidedCategory}\,(X.\mathrm{Modules})$ through the localization-monoidal chain, which transitively requires $(W\,X).\mathrm{IsMonoidal}$ --- the project's $\mathrm{instIsMonoidal\_W}$ (\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Modules/Monoidal.lean:166}). See Chapter~\ref{chap:Picard_LineBundle}'s load-bearing-disclosure paragraph and Chapter~\ref{chap:Modules_Monoidal}.
86	\end{itemize}
87	
88	Consequently, \texttt{lean\_verify} on $\PicardFunctor$ and on $\mathrm{PicardFunctor.quotMap}$ exposes \texttt{sorryAx} chains rooted at \texttt{Picard/LineBundle.lean:93,\,82} (the pull-back chain) and, indirectly, at \texttt{Modules/Monoidal.lean:166} (the monoidality-of-sheafification chain). The same chains propagate to $\PicardFunctorAb$ (Chapter~\ref{chap:Picard_FunctorAb}) and through to the Jacobian arc. The representability theorem stated above (Theorem~\ref{thm:Pic_representable}) remains deferred under the Phase~C3 exit policy and is separately downstream of these C1/C2 sorries.
89
All three edits are in place. Let me verify LaTeX validity by checking environment balance.
Begin/end blocks balance. Now writing the report.

# Blueprint Writer Report

## Slug
picard-functor-iter110

## Status
COMPLETE

## Target chapter
blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_Functor.tex

## Changes Made

- **Revised** `\uses{...}` list of the proof block of `\thm:Pic_representable` (line 38) — added `thm:SheafOfModules_pullback_tensorObj` and `thm:SheafOfModules_pullback_oneIso` so the proof block now explicitly records both halves of the named-deferred pair the closure transitively consumes. The pre-edit list contained neither oracle directly (only the umbrella `thm:Scheme_Pic_pullback`); the directive asked for both to appear, and adding both makes the dependency graph faithful to the iter-109 Lean state.
- **Revised** the "Inherited (post-C1) from Chapter~\ref{chap:Picard_LineBundle}" bullet in the "Mathlib gap" section (line 74) — replaced the stale single-oracle framing (which named only `SheafOfModules_pullback_tensorObj`) with a paired framing that records both `_tensorObj` (μ-iso, $f^*(M\otimes N)\cong f^*M\otimes f^*N$) and `_oneIso` (ε-iso, $f^*\mathcal O_Y\cong\mathcal O_X$) as siblings of the single missing `Functor.Monoidal (Scheme.Modules.pullback _)` instance. Added the simultaneous-collapse clause: when Mathlib lands `(SheafOfModules.pullback _).Monoidal`, the pair is replaced by canonical `MonoidalCategoryStruct.tensorObj`-comparison and `Monoidal.εIso` structure fields.
- **Revised** the "Pull-back gap" bullet in the "Post-C1 dependency note" subsection (line 84) — replaced the pre-iter-109 prose asserting that `Pic.pullback`, `Pic.pullback_id`, `Pic.pullback_comp` bodies are sorry-bodied with the iter-109 truth:
  - the bodies are CLOSED by hand-construction with underlying function `Functor.mapSkeleton (Scheme.Modules.pullback f)`, bundled into a `MonoidHom` whose `map_mul'` is discharged by `thm:SheafOfModules_pullback_tensorObj` and whose `map_one'` is discharged by `thm:SheafOfModules_pullback_oneIso`; the final `f*` is `Units.map` of that MonoidHom;
  - the closure transitively consumes the pair `{_tensorObj, _oneIso}` and no other Mathlib gap on the pull-back side;
  - `Pic.pullback_id` and `Pic.pullback_comp` themselves use Mathlib's existing `Scheme.Modules.pullbackId` / `pullbackComp` plus the skeleton-of-iso projection used in `Pic.pullback`; they add no further oracle dependency;
  - `PicardFunctor.quotMap` now compiles cleanly against the now-closed equalities rather than against opaque sorries;
  - all `\texttt{Picard/LineBundle.lean:93,99,105}` line-number references in the bullet body have been dropped (the prose now identifies declarations by name, not by line).

## Cross-references introduced

- `\uses{thm:SheafOfModules_pullback_tensorObj}` and `\uses{thm:SheafOfModules_pullback_oneIso}` added to the proof block of `\thm:Pic_representable`. Both labels exist in `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_LineBundle.tex` (lines 125 and 143 respectively, established in the iter-110 Picard_LineBundle.tex update).
- All cross-references in the revised bullets (`thm:SheafOfModules_pullback_tensorObj`, `thm:SheafOfModules_pullback_oneIso`, `thm:Scheme_Pic_pullback`, `def:Pic_functor`, `chap:Picard_LineBundle`) point to existing labels.

## Macros needed (if any)

None new. All math symbols used (`\Pic`, `\PicardFunctor`, `\Spec`, etc.) were already present in the chapter and use macros defined in `blueprint/src/macros/common.tex`.

## Reference-retriever dispatches (if any)

None. The directive's references (`analogies/c1-route.md`, the iter-110-updated `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_LineBundle.tex`) provided sufficient grounding; no external sources needed.

## Notes for Plan Agent

The three explicit pieces in the directive are now aligned with the iter-109 Lean state. However, two other stale references to the pre-iter-109 framing remain in the chapter and were intentionally not edited (per the directive's "the rest of the chapter is in good shape and should NOT be touched" instruction). The plan agent may want a follow-up directive to clean these up if the blueprint-reviewer flags them:

1. **NOTE comment at line 10** (above `\definition{def:Pic_functor}`) — still says "transitively consumes the sorry-bodied $\mathrm{Pic.pullback}$ (\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/LineBundle.lean:93}) ... hence transitively the named-deferred Theorem~\ref{thm:SheafOfModules_pullback_tensorObj} (\texttt{Picard/LineBundle.lean:82})". Three issues with this NOTE now:
   - "sorry-bodied $\mathrm{Pic.pullback}$" is stale — the body is now closed.
   - Only `_tensorObj` is mentioned; `_oneIso` is now also part of the transitive chain.
   - Line numbers (`LineBundle.lean:93`, `LineBundle.lean:82`, `Monoidal.lean:166`) have all shifted (or will shift) and the prose-line-number coupling is fragile.
2. **Closing paragraph at line 88** ("Consequently, \texttt{lean\_verify} on $\PicardFunctor$ ... exposes \texttt{sorryAx} chains rooted at \texttt{Picard/LineBundle.lean:93,\,82}...") — same line-number issues. Post-iter-109, the sorryAx chain on the pull-back side is rooted at `_tensorObj` and `_oneIso`, not at `Pic.pullback` itself (which is now closed). Should probably be reworded as "rooted at Theorem~\ref{thm:SheafOfModules_pullback_tensorObj} and Theorem~\ref{thm:SheafOfModules_pullback_oneIso}" (and similarly for the Monoidal.lean:166 mention).

These are local stale-state artifacts; they don't affect the dependency graph correctness (the `\uses{...}` cross-references and the body of the three edited bullets are now correct), but they will read as inconsistencies to a reviewer comparing the chapter against the Picard_LineBundle.tex revisions and the iter-109 Lean state.

## Strategy-modifying findings

None.
